user_query:
I need a ready-to-run OpenFOAM case scaffold for a steady, incompressible, turbulent 2D internal duct. Please read input/spec.yaml and input/patches.json for the physics and patch names/types, then create the full case under output/duct_case with this structure:
- output/duct_case/0/ with U, p, k, omega, and nut field files
- output/duct_case/constant/ with turbulenceProperties (kOmegaSST) and transportProperties
- output/duct_case/system/ with controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, and decomposeParDict
Requirements:
1) Use simpleFoam. Configure kOmegaSST (RAS) turbulence with appropriate wall-function patch conditions for k, omega, and nut.
2) Use exactly the patch names from input/patches.json. Treat the case as 2D with the specified thin-direction patches set to type empty in all fields.
3) Boundary conditions:
   - Inlet: U fixedValue = uniform (1 0 0); p zeroGradient
   - Outlet: p fixedValue = uniform 0; U use a suitable outlet type (e.g., pressureInletOutletVelocity or zeroGradient)
   - Walls: U noSlip; turbulence fields use wall-function types
4) Numerics:
   - fvSchemes: conservative startup (e.g., div(phi,U) Gauss upwind)
   - fvSolution: GAMG for p; smoothSolver for U, k, omega; SIMPLE with sensible under-relaxation
5) controlDict: application simpleFoam; add a yPlus function object to audit near-wall resolution
6) Decomposition: decomposeParDict with method scotch and numberOfSubdomains 4
7) Write a short summary file at output/duct_case/case_summary.md that lists: solver family, physics classification, turbulence model, patch names, confirmation of 2D empty patches, and the parallel subdomain count.
Make sure all patch names are consistent across every 0/ field file, and that the requested function object appears in controlDict. Do not put files anywhere other than output/duct_case/.
